devanagari: बाई जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ हो खिण्ण नकार उन्दुकौ हो
translation: Remember that the stubborn Two Emissaries were the Yarada who tempted the Assembly to worship the two dead ones, the Female–Eunuch–born Emissaries, who were… (:05) Bai yān naukau Yarada ar sur makhin nekki hay the Mother’s Phalanx of paired Emissary–Yarada, who killed the Priest of the Upright Horse… (:09) Bhaṇ tay kay mūkau Yarada undukau ho who invoked the protection of the Assembly’s Two Muted Ones, Yarada—the two vermin Horses… (:13) Bhan tay kay hay śan docco hum Yarada undukau ho and invoked the protection of the Assembly for worship and remembrance of the two-vermin Messenger–Yarada, who are… (:17) Bhinn Yarada oṇ dhau Yarada īśin gihi the seduced Yarada: the removed Two Serpent–Yarada Commander and Householder. (:20) Bai jūn naukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay macca hai un naukau ho The Mother’s stubborn Two Emissaries are the Little-Moon Yarada, who are the Yarada who tempted the Assembly to worship the man of the guilty two Emissaries! (:27) Baii un nau Yarada ar sur macca hay O Mother! The guilty Nine Yarada killed the mortal horse. (:30) Bundau Yarada indhaukau ho māyin naka yūnoḥ nau Yarada indhe kay hay The Little Moon Yarada Two Arrows are the deceivers of the Highest Heaven, from the Two Bands of Nine Yarada, who tempted the Assembly to worship. (:35) Ai jun nau huhu śan dvika heman dhūkau ho Remember the Mother’s stubborn Nine Celestial Chorister who graciously received twofold gold from the Two Rogues who are... (:38) the Mother’s stubborn Two Emissaries, who are the Little-Moon Yarada, who are the worn-down, worthless Two Vermin. (:45)
romanization: Bai jun naukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho khinn nakāra undukau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ शिङ्घ् गिहि मच्च है ओण् नौका यारदा इन्धौ धो धौकौ हो मच्च हि
translation: Remember the Mother’s stubborn Two Emissaries who sniffed out the Householder Man. O! Remove the Yarada Emissary [who is] of the inciters who blow the Two Winds (Rogues), who are the forsaken man. (:51)
romanization: Ai jun naukau śiṅgh gihi macca hai oṇ nauka Yarada indhau dho dhaukau ho macca hi

devanagari: बन्दी यारदा रौ ओण् धौकौ हो यारदा इन् दीदि दीदि धौ धुनौ कुहौ
translation: The female-slave Yarada’s two desires are to remove the moving ones who are the Yarada Lord’s brightly shining Two Serpents, and to drive away the Two Deceivers. (:58)
romanization: Bandi Yarada rau oṇ dhaukau ho Yarada In didi didi dhau dhunau kuhau

devanagari: भान्तौ यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ यारदा इन्धौकौ हो
translation: The two Thunderbolt Yarada conspired to worship the two Dead Ones of the Little-Moon Yarada.
romanization: Bhāntau Yarada indhe hay mūkau Yarada indhaukau ho

devanagari: बया नव नौकौ हो यारदा इन ननु नुनौ अर् सुर् शेषानाम् मच्च हि
translation: The Elder Mother’s Nine and Two Emissaries are the 13th Lunar Mansion, Hasta-Yarada — are they not the two expelled ones who killed Sesa the Serpent’s human race?
romanization: Bāyā nau naukau ho Yarada ina nanu nunau ar sur śeṣānām macca hai

devanagari: बुन्दौ यारदा इन्दका ह मूकौ हो मायिन् नह् तुन् नव यारदा इन्धात् धात उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The two Yarada-arrows of the Governing Star of the 5th Lunar Mansion of the Orion Constellation — Meissa — indeed, the two silenced were the deceiver who bound and corrupted the Nine Yarada; and the arrows are from the kindler’s semen, two rats indeed.
romanization: Bundau Yarada indakā ha mūkau ho māyin nah tun nau Yarada indhāt dhat undukau ho

devanagari: बाई जून नव यारदा अन्धात् तु नव यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Nine Yarada, blinded— and yet, the Nine Yarada themselves kindled (tempted) the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Bai jun nau Yarada andhāt tu nau Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: बाई जून नौका यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मूकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Emissary-Yarada tempted the Assembly to worship the two Muted (dead) Ones.
romanization: Bai jun nauka Yarada indhe kay hay mūkau ho

devanagari: बाई युनौ नौकौ हो मइ यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मूकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s stubborn two-band Emissaries are Mother Yarada, who tempted the Assembly to worship the two Silenced ones—so be it.
romanization: Bai yunau naukau ho mai Yarada indhe kay hay mūkau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो यारदा ईशिन् गिहि मच्च है ओ नौकौ हो
translation: The stubborn Two Emissaries are the Yarada Commander and Householder Man. Oh, the two Emissaries, so be it.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada īśin gihi macca hai oh naukau ho

devanagari: इयुन नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ हो
translation: The two Emissaries who left are the Little Moon Yarada.
romanization: Iyun naukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho

devanagari: बाई जून नौका हो यारदा अर् सुर् सुरसुर श्यामक है जौ नौका हो मच्च है
translation: The Mother’s stubborn two Emissaries are the Yarada who killed the gods & Asura’s children, oh, the two descended from the two Emissaries who are the forsaken man.
romanization: Bai jun naukau ho Yarada ar sur shurshur syāmaka hai jau naukau ho macca hi

devanagari: बन्दी कय हय् मुह् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The female slave Assembly worshipped the foolish Yarada — the two fallen vermin.
romanization: Bandi kay hay muh Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: है नौकौ माया नव नौकौ हो
translation: O the two Emissary-illusionists and the Nine and Two Emissaries…
romanization: Hai naukau māyā nau naukau ho

devanagari: शन् धूकौ हो भण् डिकी भण् टोकौ हो
translation: love the Two Rogues, (and they) call to the Gnat, (and they) call to the Two Children.
romanization: Śan dhūkau ho bhaṇ ḍikī bhan ṭokau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ शेन धेडे धुनौ नव जरा देहिन् दीदि दी ढौ नौकौ हो
translation: Remember, the two stubborn two Emissarys’ Hawk Acolytes drove away the Nine old embodied ones—whose shining was brought to decay by the two Serpent Emissaries.
romanization: Ai jun naukau śēna dhede dhunau nau jarā dehin didi di dhau naukau ho

devanagari: बाई जून नव यारदा इन्धि यून् नव यारदा इन्धौकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Nine Yarada call out ‘Ignite (Tempt)!’ to the young Nine Yarada — the Little Moons.
romanization: Bai jun nau Yarada indhi yūn nau Yarada indhūkau ho

devanagari: भण् धूकौ यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मूकौ हो  यारदा इन्दु दौ येन जेर दह् उन्दुकौ
translation: Proclaim the two rogue Yarada who tempted the Assembly to worship the two Muted Ones are the Soma-Drop Yarada, the two kindlers, the two burners, by whom the inferior Two Rats torment.
romanization: Bhaṇ dhūkau Yarada indhe kay hay mūkau ho Yarada indu dau yen jera dah undukau

devanagari: भाण्डिकी हि मुखौ हो
translation: The minor servant forsook the two mouths.
romanization: Bhāṇḍikī hi mukhau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो अर् सुर् शे सेष्यम् मच्च हय नौकौ हो
translation: Remember, the stubborn two Emissaries are the Yarada who killed I Rest, the Remainder - that mortal, and the Horse-Two Emissaries.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada ar sur śe seṣyaṃ macca hay naukau ho

devanagari: भानु यारदा इन्धे जौ नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ हो
translation: The radiant Yarada tempted their own two Emissaries — the two Little-Moon Yarada.
romanization: Bhanu Yarada indhe jau naukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho

devanagari: भण् न्यारा दह् ऊन् दूतौ सेण देह देखि हि मुखौ हो यारदा इन् दूतौ तुन् यारदा इन् दत्त-दत्त टेके हि
translation: [They] call upon the unique, burning, guilty two messengers of the cloud army—the two who saw and forsook the two Mouths, who are the Yarada Lord’s two messengers—to deceive the Yarada Lord’s anointed, handed over into Demon Teka—the Forsaken.
romanization: Bhaṇ nyārā dah ūn dūtau seṇa deh dekhi hi mukhau ho Yarada In dūtau tun Yarada In datt datt teke hi

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा एण धूकौ यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो
translation: The ritually invoked Yarada, with the two rogue Yarada, conspired to worship the two Silenced ones (dead ones).
romanization: Bhuṅ	Yarada eṇ dhūkau Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho

devanagari: भण् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: Proclaim the two Yarada rats are the Yarada who enticed the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Bhaṇ Yarada uṇḍukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भण् धूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ हो
translation: Proclaim the two Rogues are the two Yarada Rats who are the Little Moon Yarada – so be it.
romanization: Bhaṇ dhukau ho Yarada undukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो यारदा अर् सुर् शेषे मच्च हय नव जुरौ धौ उन्द् धो यारदा इन् देखि हि मखौ हो
translation: Remember, the stubborn two Emissaries are the Yarada who killed the human horse within Ananta Sesa, [with] the Nine’s two violent acts, the two Serpents, to moisten and agitate the Yarada Lord, after they had seen the forsaken two sacrifices.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada ar sur śeṣe macca hey nau jurau dhau und dho Yarada In dekhi hi makhau ho

devanagari: भण् जरा धौ उन्द् दौ यारदा इन्धि हि माया रोधौ उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्दका ह
translation: Proclaim the old Two Serpents moistened the two giver-Yarada. Burn, forsaken deception! The two hindrances—the Two Rats—are the Yarada Indakā [Meissa (Lambda Orionis), the head star of the Mṛgaśīrṣa Nakṣatra], indeed.
romanization: Bhaṇ jarā dhau und dau Yarada indhi hi māyā rodhau undukau ho Yarada indakā ha

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा इन्धौकौ हो यारदा इन्धे धेडे धेडे देऊन मुहुकैः यारदा इन्दु हो कोचौ हो माकी हि
translation: The ritually invoked Little-Moon Yarada are the Yarada who tempted the pageboys and flower girls; having given, through brief moments, the Yarada’s Soma, they are the two withering ones and are the abandoned Heaven and Earth.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada indhaukau ho Yarada indhe dhēḍe dhēḍe deūn muhukaiḥ

devanagari: बाई जून नव यारदा इन्धे जौ नौकौ हो
translation: The mother’s stubborn Nine Yarada tempted the two born from the two Emissaries.
romanization: Bai jun nau Yarada indhe jau naukau ho

devanagari: बाई अयु नौकौ हो यारदा  इन् धोडौ शेन दह् धौ नौकौ हो शन् नह् इन् नेक्की हय्
translation: The Elder Mother’s first human two Emissaries were the Yarada Lord’s two water snakes, two Falcon consuming serpent Emissaries indeed. Receive! Fasten the Lord’s uprightness to testify.
romanization: Baii ayu naukau ho Yarada In dhoḍau śēna dah dhau naukau ho śana nah In nekki hay

devanagari: बन्दी काय जरा धुन् नव जुरौ धौ धौकौ हो
translation: The old female slave assembly disrupted the Nine and two violent acts [who are] two serpent moving ones.
romanization: Bandi kay jarā dhun nau jurau dhau dhaukau ho

devanagari: बाई जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ हो बाई जनि यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s stubborn two Emissaries are the Little-Moon Yarada—her agent Yarada, two rats.
romanization: Bai jūn naukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho bai jani Yarada undhaukau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ मच्च हि
translation: Remember, the stubborn two Emissaries are the Little-Moon Yarada—the Forsaken mortal.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada indhaukau macca hi

devanagari: ऐ जून यारदा इन्धे हि मूकौ यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन् नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मच्च हि
translation: The mother’s stubborn Yarada awakened the forsaken silenced (muted) Yarada, the two rats who are the Yarada Lord’s two Emissaries who are the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship the forsaken mortal.
romanization: Ai jun Yarada indhe hi mūkau Yarada undukau ho Yarada In naukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay macca hi

devanagari: भौ यारदा इन्धौकौ हो यारदा इन्धौकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ हो
translation: The two shinning Yarada Little Moons are the two Yarada Little Moons who became the Yarada Lord having seen the two vile silenced (dead) ones.
romanization: Bhau Yarada indhaukau ho Yarada indhaukau ho Yarada In dekhi hey mūkau ho

devanagari: भण् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हि मच्च है इन् देखि हेये
translation: Declare the two Rat Yarada became the Yarada Lord after seeing the forsaken way – O [they became] a Lord after seeing the vile man.
romanization: Bhaṇ Yarada undukau ho Yarada In dekhi hi macca hai In dekhi hey

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो मायिन् नेक्की हि मच्च है
translation: The ritually invoked Yarada two Rats are the deceiver of uptightness, oh the forsaken man!
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada undukau ho māyin nekki hi macca hai

devanagari: धुन् यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ हो माया नक्की हि
translation: Resist the Yarada who tempt to worship the Two Silenced ones—the two Rats—who are deception itself!
romanization: Dhun Yarada indhe hay Mūkau ho Yarada undukau ho māyā nakki hi

devanagari: बाई जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि मोक है
translation: The Mother’s stubborn two Emissaries became the Yarada Lord (Indra) after seeing the disciple!
romanization: Bai jun naukau ho Yarada In dekhi moka hai

devanagari: बाई जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये मच्च है उन्दुकौ ईनि इज्ये रारयोः उन कोचौ हुम् मच्च है
translation: The Mother’s stubborn two Emissaries became the Yarada Lord (Indra) after seeing the vile man! The two rats [now] exist in the domain of the Lord (Agni), upon the revered authority of the paired authority domains stemming from Radha, as the guilty two withered ones -hmm – the mortal!
romanization: Bai jun Naukau ho Yarada In dekhi heye macca hai undukau ini ijye rārayoḥ un kocau hum macca hai

devanagari: ऐ जून नव यारदा इन्धे हय् माया रदौ उन्दुकौ हो
translation: Remember, the stubborn Nine Yarada ignited (conspired) to speak the deception of the two Rats with two tusks.
romanization: Ai jun nau Yarada indhe hay māyā radau undukau ho

devanagari: बाई जून नौका जरडे इन्धे हय् मऊ मूकौ जरडे ईशिन् नह् न्यारा नव उन कुहोः हो मच्च है
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Emissary among the defective one desired to worship the two Silenced ones among the Lord, to bind the unique Nine to the guilty two deceivers who are many mortals!
romanization: Bai jun nauka jaraḍe indhe hay mau mūkau jaraḍe īśin nah nyārā nau un kuhoḥ ho macca hai

devanagari: आयिन् जौ रोधौ इन् णेहे इमौ यारदा इन् नेक ओ नव यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: In compassion, the Lord – the virtuous Lord – drew near to the two descendants of the two obstacles, O, the Nine Yarada tempted the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Āyin jau rodhau In ṇehe imau Yarada In nēk, O, Nau Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: बाई जून नव यारदा इन्धे धौ नौकौ यारदा उन्दुकौ है
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Nine Yarada revived the two serpent Emissary Yarada, O, the two rats!
romanization: Bai jun Nau Yarada indhe dhau naukau Yarada undukau hai

devanagari: बाई जून नव यारदा इन्धेहि मूकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ हो
translation: O Mother! The stubborn Nine Yarada awakened the two Muted ones, [the Nine] who became the Yarada Lord (Indra) after seeing the vile two Muted ones.
romanization: Baii jun Nau Yarada indhehi mūkau ho Yarada In dekhi hey mūkau ho

devanagari: बाई जून नव कय यारदा अन्दु यारदा अर् सुर् शेषे मच्च है
translation: O, the Mother’s stubborn Nine Assembly Yarada, foot chain bound Yarada, killed Ananta Sesa’s mortal!
romanization: Bai jun Nau Kay Yarada andu Yarada ar sur śeṣe macca hai

devanagari: अये नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: I proceeded towards the two Emissaries who are the Yarada [who] ignited the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Aye naukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भण् धूकौ हो यारदा इन् धूकौ यारदा इन् ददत् उन्दुकौ हो
translation: Proclaim the two Rogues are the Yarada Lord’s two winds, the [same] Yarada Lord who gives the two Rats.
romanization: Bhaṇ dhūkau ho Yarada In dhūkau Yarada In dadat undukau ho

devanagari: भण् ति  कय हय् मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ हो माकी यारदा उन्दुकौ यारदा इन् देखि हेये
translation: Proclaim: to become moist the assembly worshiped the two silenced ones who are the Yarada, the two Rats who became the Yarada, the Two Rats of Heaven and Earth [who became] the Yarada Lord after seeing the vile [ones].
romanization: Bhaṇ ti kay hay mūkau ho Yarada undukau ho mākī Yarada undukau Yarada In dekhi hey

devanagari: भण् धौकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये जन नुनौ नव यारदा इन् नेने नय् नुनौ नव कायता खञ्जेन नादर तोकौ हो
translation: Proclaim: The two Rogues became the Yarada Lord (Indra) after seeing the vile nation, the two expelled of the Nine Yarada Lord facilitated the protection of the two expelled of the Nine scribes/clerks by the lame one’s two children.
romanization: Bhan dhūkau ho Yarada In dekhi hey jana nunau nau Yarada In nene nay nunau nau kayat khanjen nadara tokau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नव यारदा इन्धे यम् नव यारदा देह उन्दुकौ हो मच्च है इन् नेक्की हे
translation: Remember: The stubborn Nine Yarada began to control the Nine Yarada cloud like a horse, the Two Rats were mortal, O, Lord of virtue!
romanization: Ai jūnā nau Yarada indhe yam Nau Yarada dey undukau ho macca hai In nekki he

devanagari: अयु हुन् नौकौ जरा देह इन् शिङ्गे हेये मोकौ हो शरदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The 1st mankind performed ritual offerings – the Two Emissaries and the Old Cloud Lord – in the tree to worship the vile two cast off skins who are the newer two vermin.
romanization: Ayu hun naukau jarā dey In śiṅge hey mokau ho śārada undukau ho

devanagari: बिन जेर दातौ जेर दह् इन्धे हेये मुखौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The son of [those] overcome by the two that are divided, overcome by temptation to worship the vile two muted ones, the two Rat Yarada.
romanization: Bin jera dātau jera dah indhe hey mukau ho Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: वेंटे कय नव नौकौ हो कयर दह् इन्धे कय हय् मच्छेण जेर लोकान् श्येनेन तुन् नौकौ हो
translation: The coiled assembly – regarding the Nine and the two Emissaries – became miserable after being tempted to worship, the worlds overcame them by means of the Fish, and by means of the Hawk the two Emissaries were deceived.
romanization: Vēṇṭe kay nau naukau ho kayara dah indhe kay hay macchen jera lokan śyenenā tun naukau ho

devanagari: अयु सुर शेषे शेम मच्च है जौ नौकौ हो
translation: The 1st Mankind killed Ananta Sesa the Hundred’s mankind, the two descended from the two Emissaries!
romanization: Ayu sur śeṣ śem macca hai jau naukau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हिड्ड धौ उन नौकौ हो माई जरडे इन् देखि हेये
translation: Remember, the stubborn two Emissaries, the two Serpents of Hidda (a place near Jalalabad Afghanistan), those guilty two Emissaries are the Mother among the defective ones – [who became] the Lord (Indra) after seeing the vile [one].
romanization: Ai jun naukau Hiḍḍa dhau un naukau ho māī jaraḍe In dekhi hey

devanagari: भण् तोकौ हो इन् यारदा हेये मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ
translation: Proclaim the two children became the Yarada Lord after seeing the contemptible two Silenced ones who are the two Yarada Rats.
romanization: Bhaṇ tokau ho Yarada In dekhi hey mūkau ho Yarada undukau

devanagari: बिन यारदा तोकौ कोचौ यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ
translation: The son of the Yarada, the two children, two withering Yarada, ignited to worship the two silenced ones who are the two Yarada Rats.
romanization: Bin Yarada tokau kocau Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: बाई यून् ओयर ओण् ऊन् जुरौ धौ इन्धे हय् मच्च हेये मूकौ हो माया रदः इन्धे हेये मूकौ हो मच्च है
translation: The Mother’s young avatar removed the guilty tow violent acts, the two Serpents that tempted to worship the way of the vile two Silenced ones [who] are the deceptive tusk [who] ignited the vile two Silenced ones who are that man!
romanization: Bai yūn oyar oṇ ūn jurau dhau indhe hay macca hey mūkau ho maya radaḥ indhe hey mūkau ho macca hai

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ हो
translation: Remember the stubborn two Emissaries became the Yarada Lord after seeing the vile two Silenced ones.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada In dheki hey mukau ho.

devanagari: बाई जून नव नव  यारदा इन्धे हय् मोहौ यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Nine, the Nine Yarada, tempted to worship the two Hallucinations, the two Yarada Rats.
romanization: Bai jun nau nau Yarada indhe hay mohau Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: बाई जन् जरा लोकौ हो मच्च है इन्धे यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s offspring, the two decaying worlds, are the mortal that tempted the Yarada [to worship] the two Leprosies.
romanization: Bai jan jarā lokau ho macca hai indhe Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: भेंडी ज उन्दुकौ यारदा ईशिन् घि हेय
translation: A Bhendi tree born from two leprosies, the Yarada Commander [and] the vile Summer.
romanization: Bhēṇḍī ja undukau Yarada īśin ghi hēy

devanagari: बाई जून नव यारदा उन्दुकौ हो माया रयीन् नेक्की हय
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Nine Yarada and Two Rats are the illusory treasures of the upright Horse.
romanization: Bai jun nau Yarada undukau ho māyā rayin nekki hay

devanagari: बुंत अयर धा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्धे हय् मोहौ यारदा इन्धे हय् केकय मूकौ हो
translation: A disguise [as of] the Ageless [ones] worn by the two Rats who are the Yarada who tempted to worship the two Delusions which prevent the discernment of truth, the [same] Yarada that tempted the Kekaya Tribe to worship the two Silenced ones.
romanization: Bunt ayara dhā undukau ho Yarada indhe hay mohua Yarada indhe hay Kekaya mūkau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून टोकौ हो माईक हय मच्च है मुण् यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: O, remember the stubborn two children are the fraudulent Horse man, [which] pledged themselves to the Yarada [who] tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Ai jun ṭokau ho maika hay macca hai muṇ Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भुङ् टोकौ हो यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा इन् दूतौ कोण नौकौ यारदा इन् तय् हय
translation: The two children are the Yarada who tempted [the assembly] to worship the two Silenced ones are the Yarada who were tempted to worship the two Silenced ones, [they] are the Yarada Lord’s Two Messengers. Which Emissary? The Yarada Lord’s protecting Horse.
romanization: Bhuṅ ṭokau ho Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho Yarada In dūtau koṇ nauka Yarada In tay hay.

devanagari: ऐ जून यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मूकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हि
translation: Remember, the stubborn Yarada tempted the Assembly to worship the two Silenced ones, these same Yarada tempted the assembly, indeed.
romanization: Ai jūn Yarada indhe kay hay mūkau ho Yarada indhe kay hi

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा इन् धोडौ यारदा ऊन् नौकौ हो मच्च है
translation: The Yarada Lord’s two Water Snakes, the Yarada Lord’s wedding ceremony children, and the guilty two Emissaries are mortal (subject to death)!
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada In dhoḍau Yarada ūn naukau ho macca hai

devanagari: ऐ जून यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ यरादी इन्धे हि
translation: Remember, the stubborn Yarada [who] tempted to worship the two Silenced ones are those Yarada, the two Leprosies, the Hereditary Office that tempted, indeed.
romanization: Ai jūn Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho Yarada undukau yāradī indhe hi

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: The Yarada, the two Leprosies, are the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada undukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भान्ते कय हय् मूकौ यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ यारदा इन् धोडौ टोटौ यारदा किञ्चित् जौ मूकौ हो
translation: The Moon Assembly (Lunar Mansions) worshipped the two Silenced ones. The Yarada [who] kindled the worshiping of the two Silenced ones [were] the Yarada Lord’s two water snakes, the two Yarada children somewhat born from the two Silenced ones.
romanization: Bhānte kay hay mūkau Yarada indhe hay mūkau Yarada In dhoḍau ṭoṭau Yarada kiñcit jau mūkau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून यारदा अन्दु यारदा इषय शेषे मूकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: Remember the stubborn Yarada, elephant foot chain bound Yarada was eager within Sesa’s two Silenced ones, [the same Yarada] are the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Ai jun Yarada andu Yarada iṣay śeṣe mūkau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: बाई जुना नव यारदा इन्धौकौ यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मूकौ हो
translation: The Mother’s stubborn Nine Yarada and the two Little Moon Yarada tempted the Assembly to worship the two dead ones, indeed.
romanization: Bai jun nau Yarada indhaukau Yarada indhe kay hay mūkau ho

devanagari: ऐ ओण् यारदा उर् शूर् शे सेष्यम् मच्च है ज जरडा उन्दुकौ यारदा इन्धे कय हय् मारद इन्धे कय हय्
translation: Remember the removal of the Yarada who proceeded to kill, [who is named] I Rest [or] the Remainder, the mortal! Born from the decay of the two Rat Yarada who tempted the Assembly to worship death giving – who tempted the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Ai oṇ Yarada ur śūr śe seṣyaṃ macca hai ja jarada undukau Yarada indhe kay hay mārada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा ध्यान नौका यारदा इन् भूकौ हो मच्च हय
translation: The Yarada remembered that the two Emissaries and the Yarada Lord’s two Darknesses are the mortal horse.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada dhyān naukau Yarada In bhūkau ho macca hay

devanagari: बन्धौ कुहौ यारदा अर् सुर् शे शेष्यम् मच्च है जौ नौकौ न्यारा दह् इन्धे कय हय्
translation: The two pledged deceivers, Yarada, praised the killings of I Rest The Remainder’s mortal; these two who descended from the two Emissaries – separately incited and tempted the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Bandhau kuhau Yarada ar sur śe sesyam macca hai jau naukau nyārā dah indhe kay hay

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो ऐ जून नव यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि मूकौ हो
translation: The Yarada two Rats are, remember too the stubborn Nine Yarada, [those who] tempted to worship the two Silenced ones and became the Yarada Lord after seeing the two Silenced ones.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada undukau ho ai jun nau Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho Yarada In dekhi mūkau ho

devanagari: भान्ते कय हय् मूकौ यारदा ईशिन् गिहि
translation:
In the Moon Assembly (Nakshatra) [they] worshipped the two Silenced ones:
         1) The Yarada Commander 2) Householder (Matriarch)
romanization: Bhānte kay hay mūkau Yarada isin gihi

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: The Yarada Lord, after seeing the vile two Silenced ones, became the Two Yarada lepers.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada In dekhi hey mūkau ho Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: भान्ते कय हय् मूकौ यारदा ईशिन् गिहि मूकौ यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ
translation: In the Moon Assembly [they] worshiped the two Silenced ones, the Yarada Commander and Householder (Matriarch), the two Silenced ones, [because of] the Yarada Lord having seen the vile two Silenced ones.
romanization: Bhānte kay hay mūkau Yarada isin higi mūkau yarada In dekhi hey mūkau

devanagari: भण् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ हो
translation: Declare the Yarada two Rats are the Yarada who tempted to worship the two Silenced ones and became the Yarada Lord after seeing the vile two Silenced ones.
romanization: Bhaṇ Yarada undukau ho Yarada indhe hay mūkau ho Yarada In dekhi hey mūkau ho

devanagari: भान्ते कय हय् मुह् यारदा उर् शूर् शेष्यम् मच्च है
translation: In the Moon Assembly the confused Yarada worshiped the murdering of the Remainder’s Mortals.
romanization: Bhānte kay hay muh Yarada ur śūr śeṣyam macca hai

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हेये मूकौ हो यारदा उन्दुकौ हो
translation: Remember the stubborn two Emissaries became the Yarada Lord after seeing the vile two Silenced ones and became the two Leprosy Yarada.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada In dekhi hey mūkau ho Yarada undukau ho

devanagari: भान्ते कय हय् माया रोधौ ऊन् कोचौ चौ सेण गिहि
translation: In the Moon Assembly [they] worshipped the deceit, the two obstacles, the two guilty withering morally corrupt falcon Matriarch.
romanization: Bhante kay hay māyā rodhau ūn kocau cau sen gihi

devanagari: महान 	ओण् जनधा इन्धे ओण् नव यारदा अङ्घो कोचौ यारदा इन् गिहि
translation: The epic removal of the Provider ignited the removal of the Nine Yarada, ugh! The two withering [ones], the Yarada Lord and Matriarch.
romanization: Mahān oṇ janadhā indhe oṇ nau Yarada aṅgho kocau Yarada In gihi

devanagari: भण् तोकौ हो यारदा इषय शेषे मूकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: Proclaim the two children are Yarada active within Ananta Sesa’s two Silenced ones [the children are] the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Bhaṇ tokau ho Yarada iṣay śeṣe mūkau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा उन्दुकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: The Yarada two Rats are the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada undukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भुङ् यारदा अर् सुर् शे शेष्यम् मच्च है जे ये णूणो यारदा इने नेने नेणणे नौकौ हो
translation: The Yarada murdered I Rest, the remainder’s human. Oh—those who are the wicked ones: the Yarada in the Sun, who lead in ignorance—the two Emissaries indeed.
romanization: Bhuṅ Yarada ar sur śe śeṣyam macca hai jē ye nuno Yarada ine nene nēṇaṇē naukau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नौकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: Remember the two Emissaries are the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Ai jun naukau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: भान्ते कय हय् मूकौ यारदा इन्धे हय् नुनौ उन कोचौ माया रदः इन् देखि हय
translation: In the Moon Assembly [they] worshiped the two Silenced ones, Yarada temted to worship the two expelled, the two guilty withering fraudulent tusk Lord, having seen  the horse
romanization: Bhānte kay hay mūkau Yarada indhe hay nunau un kocau māyā radaḥ In dekhi hay

devanagari: ऐ जून नव यारदा अर् सुर् शे शेष्यम् मच्च है ज नुनौ जरडी इन् देखि हय मूकौ हो
translation: Remember the Nine Yarada killed I Rest the Remainder’s (He Who Remains) mortals! Born from the two expelled feeble Lord, after seeing the two Horse Silenced ones.
romanization: Ai jun nau Yarada ar sur śe śeṣyam macca hai ja nunau jaradi In dekhi hay mūkau ho

devanagari: बाणी यारदा उन नौकौ हो यारदा इन् नेक्की हय
translation: The utterance Yarada and guilty two Emissaries were the Yarada Lord & upright Horse.
romanization: Bāṇī Yarada un naukau ho Yarada In nekki hay

devanagari: भुङ् धूकौ हो बाई यारदा उन्दुकौ
translation: The two winds (rogues) are the Mother Yarada’s two Rats.
romanization: Bhuṅ dhūkau ho bai Yarada undukau

devanagari: बाई जन् ओ यारदा इन् आयिन् उन यारदा अनुकैः अये उन ओका यारदा देह इन्धे हय् मूकौ हो
translation: The Mother birthed you Yarada! The Lord approaches the guilty Yarada through the greedy ones. I am coming guilty naked Yarada cloud who tempted [the Moon Assembly] to worship the two Silenced ones.
romanization: Bai jan O Yarada In āyin un Yarada anukaiḥ aye un ōk Yarada deh indhe hay mūkau ho

devanagari: आय उन जेर इ शेषे शेषे शेषे मोहौ इ शेषे शेषे शेषे
translation: The 11th House, guilty, reduced by disease, entirely in the Domain of Sesa, two mental delusions, entirely in the Domain of Sesa.
romanization: Āya un jera I śeṣe śeṣe śeṣe mohau I śeṣe śeṣe śeṣe

devanagari: बाई याराः ओरा उर् शूर् शे शेष्यम् मच्च है
translation: The Mother’s friend proceeded like rushing water to kill Sesa’s mankind…
romanization: Bai Yārāḥ or ur śūr śe śeṣyam macca hai

devanagari: शन् ईळांना यारदा ईशिन् गिहि
translation: To honor the Commander & Matriarch during the times of the Yarada.
romanization: Śan īḷānn Yarada isin gihi

devanagari: बुन्द यारदा उन्दुकौ हो बक है जन् जरतोः
translation: The lower Yarada two Rats are rogue! Born of the two aged ones, the guilty immature Yarada [who] tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Bund Yarada undukau ho baka hai jan jaratoḥ

devanagari: बुङ्ग् यारदा उन्दुकौ यारदा इन्धे हेय मूकौ हो यारदा इन् देखि हय मूकौ हो
translation: Forsake the Yarada, the two Leprosy Yarada, [who] ignited the rejected two Mutes who became the Yarada Lord after seeing the two muted Horse.
romanization: Buṅg Yarada undukau Yarada indhe hey mūkau ho Yarada In dekhi hay mūkau ho

devanagari: भानून् यारदा इने बुङ्ग् ओ यारदा इन्द् ओकौ हो यारदा ईशिन् गिहि मूकौ हो
translation: The Solar Authorities – Yarada within the sun, forsook, oh, the Yarada’s two powerful Houses are [that of] the Yarada Commander and Matriarch, the two silenced ones.
romanization: Bhānūn Yarada ine buṅg O Yarada ind okau ho Yarada isin gihi mūkau ho

devanagari: ऐ जून नव यारदा इन्धे हय् मौ यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ यारदा इन्धौकौ हो
translation: Remember the stubborn Nine Yarada tempted to worship the two Moon Yarada, tempted to worship the two silent Yarada – the little Moons.
romanization: Ai jūn nau Yarada indhe hay mau Yarada indhe hay mūkau Yarada indhaukau ho

devanagari: बाई जुना ओ यारदा इषय शे शेष्यम् मोकौ हो यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: The Mother’s stubborn, oh, the eager Yarada, He Who Remain’s two cast off skins are the Yarada who tempted the Assembly to worship.
romanization: Bai jūn O Yarada iṣay śe śeṣyam mokau ho Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: ऐ जून यारदा इषय शेषे शे मोकौ हो यारदा इन्धे हय् मूकौ यारदा इन्धे कय हय्
translation: Remember the stubborn eager Yarada, Anata Sesa’s two cast off skins are the Yarada who tempted to worship the two Muted ones, the Yarada who tempted the assembly to worship.
romanization: Ai jūn Yarada iṣay śeṣe śe mokau ho Yarada indhe hay mūkau Yarada indhe kay hay

devanagari: बाईने ओण् ए यारदा इषय शेषे मोकौ हो यारदा इन्धौ यारदा अर् सुर् शेषे शेष्यम् मच्च है
translation: Alas! The eager Yarada the two skins who were removed from upon the Head of Sesa by the Mother are the two Yarada tempters, the Yarada who murdered Anata Sesa’s mankind!
romanization: Bāīne oṇ E Yarada iṣay śeṣe mokau ho Yarada indhau Yarada ar sur śeṣe śeṣyam macca hai

devanagari: ऐ जून ओयर ढ उन ओयर ढ इने रुङ् यारदा इन् उन अङ्गून् गंगा इङ्गनैः ज्ञानिन् यारदा उन नौकौ हो
translation: Remember the stubborn guilty Avatar Serpent within the sun, the Avatar Serpent injured the Yarada Lord, the guilty birds and sacred river by shaking (dislodging) the Mercury Yarada and the guilty Emissaries.
romanization: Ai jūn oyara ḍha un oyara ḍha ine ruṅ Yarada In un aṅgūn gaṅg iṅganaiḥ jñānin Yarada un naukau ho
